mission: Build a NeurIPS-level research system for exact game-structure supervision, learned agency representations, computational aesthetics, and verifier-backed discovery in chess-derived domains.
operating_principles: Exact labels outrank plausible labels; unsupported positions must fail loudly.
Every ML claim must be paired with a verifier, baseline, ablation, and held-out generalization test.
The paper is the product: code, datasets, metrics, and discovery artifacts exist to support falsifiable claims.
Aesthetics must be operationalized as measurable structure, then illustrated with verified positions.
Citations are included only when they support a concrete claim, construction, baseline, or related-work comparison.

global_gates.standard: Every generated artifact records code commit, generator config, random seed, domain definition, label schema version, and verifier version.
No dataset row is released without exact-verifier status, unsupported/error status, or explicit exclusion reason.
Every reported model result is compared against scalar-supervised, material/handcrafted, and simple architecture baselines where applicable.
Every main result includes at least one out-of-distribution split: composition, board scale, motif family, or value class.
Every showcased position has a machine-checkable certificate: legal position, exact value/label, agency metrics, and minimality or compactness score.

agents.mission: Own the central hypothesis, claim table, evaluation ladder, and NeurIPS-readiness bar.
verification: Every claim links to a dataset, experiment, theorem, or verified gallery artifact
No paper claim depends on placeholder labels or unverified generator output

agents.mission: Formalize the restricted game domains, decomposition assumptions, agency metrics, and aesthetic scores.
deliverables: Domain definitions for the first exact benchmark
Proof obligations for decomposition and value summation
verification: Definitions are executable or backed by tests
Each metric has known positive and negative control positions

agents.mission: Implement exact finite-game algebra, canonicalization, thermographs, and value serialization.
verification: cargo test
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
Property tests for dominated/reversible option removal and value equivalence

agents.mission: Own board partitioning, locked-structure detection, and decomposition certificates.
verification: Partition tests on synthetic barriers and chess positions
All decomposition claims emit machine-checkable certificates

agents.mission: Own exact enumeration and retrograde labeling for the first constrained domain.
verification: Every generated predecessor can legally reach its child
Expansion caps never consume work beyond the cap
Small domains exhaustively match brute-force search

agents.mission: Turn exact generators into versioned datasets with splits, metadata, compression, and error accounting.
verification: No silent row drops
Every row validates against the label schema
Dataset regeneration is deterministic from manifest

agents.mission: Train and evaluate scalar, structured, and concept-bottleneck models against exact labels.
deliverables: Representation probes for agency and temperature
verification: Every run logs config, seed, commit, dataset version, and metric schema
Scalar-supervised controls are trained and evaluated under matched compute

agents.mission: Use learned models plus exact verification to find compact, surprising, agency-rich positions.
verification: Every gallery position has exact labels and metric certificate
Generated positions are checked against training-set leakage

agents.mission: Attack every label, decomposition, metric, and experimental claim before it reaches the paper.
verification: Each phase has at least one adversarial test suite
Every failed assumption is either fixed, scoped out, or documented

agents.mission: Continuously pressure-test the framing, related work, novelty, and reviewer story.
verification: No citation appears unless it supports a specific paper claim
Every figure has a corresponding experiment or certificate

phases.goal: Make the program executable: claim table, task ownership, gates, and first constrained domain.

tasks.done_when: Each proposed NeurIPS claim has required evidence, baseline, and failure mode.
The first benchmark domain is narrow enough for exact labels and broad enough for meaningful generalization.
Global gates are translated into CI-ready checks or explicit manual certificates.

exit_criteria: Every agent has a first deliverable
No placeholder-label path remains in the data pipeline

phases.goal: Produce exact, reproducible labels for a constrained but nontrivial domain.

tasks.done_when: Golden integer, dyadic, switch, fuzzy, and simple infinitesimal fixtures pass.
Every decomposition returns assumptions, components, and counterexample checks.
Small domains match brute force and emit provenance for every label.

exit_criteria: Dataset v0 can be generated with exact labels
Verifier red-team has at least 20 counterexamples or negative controls
All engine crates pass tests, clippy, and formatting

phases.goal: Release an internal benchmark with train/test/OOD splits and agency/aesthetic metrics.

tasks.done_when: Rows validate exact label, domain, split, metrics, generator config, and verifier status.
Agency and aesthetic metrics have formulas, controls, and expected ranges.
At least composition, motif-family, and value-class OOD splits are defined.

phases.goal: Show whether exact structure supervision learns agency and temperature beyond scalar baselines.

tasks.done_when: Scalar, material/feature, CNN, and structured models run on matched splits.
Probes measure agency, temperature, decomposition, and value class information.
Main claim has positive result, baseline comparison, and failure analysis.

exit_criteria: At least one main hypothesis survives matched baselines
All experiments are reproducible from manifests
Failure modes are documented rather than hidden

phases.goal: Use models to discover verified agency-rich positions and turn results into a submission-ready story.

tasks.done_when: Candidate search improves verified aesthetic/agency scores over random and heuristic baselines.
Every showcased position has exact labels, metrics, and leakage checks.
Every section maps to evidence, figures, and a defensible novelty claim.

exit_criteria: Discovery results are verified and baseline-beating
Paper outline has all required evidence slots filled or explicitly marked as blocked

first_sprint.task: Write the first constrained domain spec with exact legality, board assumptions, move rules, terminal conditions, and excluded cases.
first_sprint.success_signal: A verifier can reject unsupported positions before solving.

first_sprint.task: Create a golden-value suite for simple finite games and the value classes the first benchmark will expose.
first_sprint.success_signal: Tests fail if integer, dyadic, fuzzy, or switch simplification regresses.

first_sprint.task: Define the dataset row schema and manifest fields before generating more data.
first_sprint.success_signal: The orchestrator can emit successes, errors, exclusions, and provenance uniformly.

first_sprint.task: Create the initial claim table with required baselines, metrics, and falsification conditions.
first_sprint.success_signal: Every planned experiment has a reason it belongs in a NeurIPS paper.

first_sprint.task: Build an initial bank of decomposition and label counterexamples.
first_sprint.success_signal: At least ten known-bad examples are represented as tests or fixture rows.
